english: instrument like a hautboy, used in sacred ceremonies; rkaṅ-gliṅ (rkang gling) lit. a fife made of the human femural bone, but sometimes also of metal.

song, tune, mostly, though not always, of a profane nature, opp. to religious hymns; glu-dbyaṅs (glu dbyangs), glu-sgrá (glu sgra), id.; the word is also used of the singing of birds; glu-ćuṅ (glu chung) a little song, ditty, hummed by a person Glr.; glu-rés (glu res) alternate song; glu-gar-rtséd-po (glu gar rtsed po) rejoicings of every kind Glr.; glu lén-pa (glu len pa) B., *lu gyáb-pa* (*lu gyab pa*) C., *táṅ-će* (*tang ce*) W., to sing.

a ransom, a thing given as a ransom, sróg-gi glud (srog gi glud) a ransom for one's life Lex.; ḱoí glúd-du lug brgya gsód-pa (kho'i glud du lug brgya gsod pa), to slaughter a hundred sheep as a ransom Mil.; *lú'-la taṅ* (*lu' la tang*) C. he is made an expiator, a scape-goat; *mi-lu* (*mi lu*) C. in a special sense: a man's image which in his stead is cast away in the gtór-ma (gtor ma): therefore *ḱo mi-lú' yin* (*kho mi lu' yin*) C. he is a curse, an anathema, one deserving to be cursed (ni.f.).
boiled barley, wheat, or rice, used instead of malt in brewing beer (not for food).
1. Glr. 60. a small uncultivated island, = gliṅ-ka (gling ka) (Ld. *zal* (*zal*)). - 2. n. of the capital of Ladak, usually sle (sle).
n. of a distemper Cs.; involuntary discharge from the bowels, or of urine Sch.

(Cs. glégs-ma (glegs ma)) table, board, plate; zȧṅs-kyi glégs-bu (zangs kyi glegs bu) copper-plate Tár. 26, 10; glegs-bám (glegs bam) book, also dpé-ća glegs-bám (dpe cha glegs bam) Glr.; glegs-bám maṅ-po bźéṅs-so (glegs bam mang po bzhengs so) he made a present of, dedicated, many books (for the use of a temple); glegs-śiṅ (glegs shing) the wooden boards which in a Tibetan book supply the binding; glegs-tág (glegs thag) a thong etc. fastened round a book; glegs-ćáb (glegs chab) a buckle, clasp, or ring attached to this thong. — sgó-glegs (sgo glegs) the pannel of a door; *núm-lag* (*num lag*) writing-tablet, a small board, blackened, greased, and strewed over with scraped chalk, on which the school-children write with wood-pencils.

pf. gleṅs (glengs) to say, talk, converse, gtam (-du) gléṅ-ba (gtam (-du) gleng ba) id., resp. gsuṅ gléṅ-ba (gsung gleng ba); 'lám-la ma tar' źes gléṅs-nas ('lam la ma thar' zhes glengs nas) as word was sent: 'the road is not passable!' Glr.; gtam gléṅ-ba ni bdág-gis byas (gtam gleng ba ni bdag gis byas) I have made this speech S.O.; gćig gléṅ gnyis gleṅ rim-pas mčéd-de btsún-moi bár-du gléṅ-źiṅ (gcig gleng gnyis gleng rim pas mched de btsun mo'i bar du gleng zhing) the rumour spreading from one to the other, until it came before the queen Pth.; čós-kyi sgrog-gléṅ byéd-pa (chos kyi sgrog gleng byed pa), (resp. mdzád-pa (mdzad pa)), to preach Glr.; gros-gléṅ (gros gleng) council, consultation, perh. also disputation. Comp. and deriv. gléṅ-brjód (gleng brjod), gléṅ-mo (gleng mo) sbst. conversation, discourse, lecture, gléṅ-brjód ma maṅ dar-gćig gson (gleng brjod ma mang dar gcig gson) listen a little to a short discourse Mil.; čós-gtam gléṅ-mo byed (chos gtam gleng mo byed) let us converse on religious subjects Mil.; gléṅ-mo (gleng mo) the act of speaking, opp. to yi-ge (yi ge), the act of writing, the written letter etc. Lex. — gléṅ-gźi (gleng gzhi) 1. the subject of a discourse Cs. 2. table of contents, index S.O. and elsewh. 3. place, scene, of a conversation or discourse Stg. frq. — gléṅ-ba-po (gleng ba po), gléṅ-mo-mḱan (gleng mo mkhan) a story-teller Cs.; gleṅ-₀bum (gleng 'bum) 'a hundred thousand stories', title of a book, Sch.

1. B. and C. stupid, foolish, glen lkugs bkol-spyód-kyi sdug-bsṅál (glen lkugs bkol spyod kyi sdug bsngal) the misery of stupidity, of dumbness and of servitude (the state of animals) Thgr.; byol-sóṅ-las kyaṅ glén-po (byol song las kyang glen po) more stupid than a brute Mil.; fool, kyód-rnams re glen (khyod rnams re glen) fools that you are all of you Dzl.; often in the sense of 'fool' in the Bible, = the wicked, the ungodly: glén-pa gti-mug-ćan (glen pa gti mug can) infatuated fools Dzl. 22, 9 = profaners of holy things; *len-nág* (*len nag*) W. id.; *len-nág-gi pé-ra* (*len nag gi pe ra*) foolish talk. — 2. W.: idle, lazy, dull, imbecile, e.g. a sickly child, an animal affected with a disease (opp. to *tám-pa, śáṅ-po* (*tam pa, shang po*) being in good health, active, lively).
pf. glebs (glebs), to make flat, plain Cs., léb-mor gleb (leb mor gleb) Lex.
to press, squeeze; to crush, squash Stg., C.
(Ld. ཟློ་ *zlo* (*zlo*)), resp. gźogs (gzhogs), 1. the side, esp. of the body, glos páb-pa (glos phab pa) to lie down on one's side (lit. by
